title: Accessible Checkbox Group

statement.markdown:
## Accessible Checkbox Group

Create an accessible checkbox group using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Ensure that the group is usable with a screen reader and keyboard navigation.

### Requirements
1. Use an unordered list (`<ul>`) to contain the checkboxes.
2. Ensure that each checkbox has a unique `id` and a descriptive label associated with it.
3. Use the `role="checkbox"` attribute along with `aria-checked` so custom checkbox elements are announced correctly and stay focusable with a keyboard.
4. If hiding the native checkbox input, keep it accessible (e.g. visually hidden, not `display: none`) so it's still reachable by assistive technology, or manage state entirely via `aria-checked` on the custom control.

constraints: {"type":"html","message":"Your HTML should not contain any syntax errors."}
{"type":"css","message":"Your CSS should not contain any syntax errors and should use at least 2 Tailwind CSS classes."}
{"type":"js","message":"Your JavaScript should not contain any syntax errors and should be functional."}
